hypothesis: The pre-push gate (6 layers: PHP lint, structural markers, expanded URLs, WP runtime scan, PHP 8.x grep, PHPCompatibility PHPCS) is defined as a manual checklist in experiments/surfaces/deploy-reliability/current.md. Zero actual gate executions have been observed in any experiment window (0 deploys in 8 of 9 windows). The gate may be bypassed on any push where the developer forgets to run it, or finds it too cumbersome. Adding a committed .githooks/pre-push script to the reyco-marine repo that automates steps 2-4.75 (installed via git config core.hooksPath .githooks) will cause the gate to fire automatically on every push attempt — eliminating the manual bypass risk and producing the first real empirical gate execution data. Hypothesis: after hook install, future 48h windows will contain at least one observed gate execution, enabling genuine reliability measurement.
measurement: Percentage of deploys where CI smoke test passes on first push (track via git log + CI run history)
changes_description: Added .githooks/pre-push automation script to reyco-marine repo. Script automates 6-layer gate: PHP lint, structural marker smoke (6 URLs), WP runtime error scan, PHP 8.x grep, PHPCompatibility PHPCS. Install: git config core.hooksPath .githooks. Committed as 3488631 on feat/deploy-gate-pre-push-hook branch. Pending PR + merge to master.
